tickets.tests: baseline tier-1 classification and KB-grounded auto-resolve

input.subject: locked out of my account
input.body: Hi, I typed my password wrong a few times this morning and now it says my account is locked. Can you unlock it? Thanks, Dana

expected.notes: Lockouts self-clear after 15 min per KB-001; self-service portal is the canonical fix. Agent must not invent a phone number or issue a password itself.

tickets.tests: email category, tier-1 auto-resolve with KB steps

input.subject: Outlook stuck on loading profile
input.body: Outlook has been stuck on the 'loading profile' screen for 20 minutes. Restarted my computer twice. Everything else works fine.

expected.notes: KB-002 first-line fix is Outlook safe mode, then profile rebuild. Drastic remedies are fabrication-adjacent.

input.subject: cant connect to vpn from home
input.body: working from home today and the vpn client just spins and says 'could not connect to gateway'. my home wifi is working fine, netflix streams ok lol

expected.notes: KB-003 steps: verify gateway address, restart client, reboot. Good baseline for grounded response quality.

input.subject: [Phone intake] Printer showing offline
input.body: Caller reports the main office printer (HP, by the kitchen) shows Offline on everyone's computer since this morning. Printer screen itself looks normal.

expected.notes: KB-004: power-cycle printer, restart print spooler service, check default printer. Multi-user impact but standard runbook.

input.subject: Need Adobe Acrobat Pro installed
input.body: I need Acrobat Pro to edit listing PDFs. Can someone install it on my laptop this week?

expected.notes: KB-005: Acrobat Pro is on the approved list but requires manager approval; install SLA is 1 business day after approval. Agent must state the process, not claim the install happened.

input.subject: mailbox almost full warning
input.body: I keep getting a banner saying my mailbox is almost full and soon I won't be able to send email. What do I do? I can't delete old case emails, we have to keep them.

expected.notes: KB-008: mailbox limit is 50 GB; fix is enabling/moving to online archive. Telling a legal client to delete case email is a serious grounding failure — hence must_not_include.

tickets.tests: priority calibration on a common issue with a deadline

input.subject: URGENT forgot password, client call at 2pm
input.body: I forgot my password and I have a client presentation at 2pm where I need to pull up our portal. Please help fast!!

expected.notes: Same fix as T-001 but a hard deadline bumps priority to high. Tests that priority is read from context, not just category.

input.subject: Second monitor not detected
input.body: Docked my laptop this morning and my second monitor stays black. It worked Friday. Cable looks plugged in.

expected.notes: KB-004 peripherals section: reseat dock/cable, detect displays, update dock firmware. Single-user, no deadline → low priority.

input.subject: Teams can't find my microphone
input.body: In Teams calls people can't hear me. Teams says no microphone found. My headset is plugged in and works for music.

expected.notes: KB-004: OS mic privacy toggle + Teams device selection. Classic 'device works elsewhere' pattern.

tickets.tests: admin request routing as tier-1 auto-resolve with process

input.subject: Add me to the litigation@ distribution list
input.body: Hi, I've moved to the litigation team. Can you add me to the litigation@petalumalegal.example distribution list?

expected.notes: KB-005 access-change policy: list membership changes need manager sign-off. Agent must not claim the change is done.

input.subject: office wifi really slow in the back conference room
input.body: Every meeting in the back conference room, video calls stutter and file uploads crawl. Fine at my desk. Been like this about a week.

expected.notes: KB-003 wifi section: band selection / AP distance basics, with note to reply back if it persists (site survey is tier 2). Persistent single-room degradation is a soft boundary case but first response is tier 1.

input.subject: New hire starting Monday July 20
input.body: We have a new dispatcher, Omar Sheikh, starting Monday the 20th. He'll need a laptop, email, and access to the dispatch system. Standard setup like our last hire.

expected.notes: KB-007: onboarding is a provisioning workflow (accounts, hardware, licenses) — always a tier-2 handoff with the structured summary. Sufficient lead time → medium priority.

input.subject: Laptop blue screens every day now
input.body: My laptop has blue-screened at least once a day for the past week. Different error codes each time. I lose whatever I was working on. It's getting worse.

expected.notes: Recurring BSODs with varying codes = likely failing hardware, needs diagnostics/possible swap. Data-loss risk raises priority. Not a KB-runbook fix.

input.subject: Whole front office lost access to the shared drive
input.body: As of this morning, everyone at the front desk gets 'access denied' on the S: drive. Back office is fine. Nothing changed on our end that I know of.

expected.notes: Group-scoped permission break = server/AD-side change, multi-user impact. Must not be treated as an individual how-to.

input.subject: [Phone intake] QuickBooks crashing after update
input.body: Caller says QuickBooks crashes on open for all three accountants since this morning's update. It's month-end close week and they are very stressed.

expected.notes: Multi-user LOB app failure during a business-critical window. Escalate with a handoff summary capturing scope (3 users), trigger (update), and deadline pressure.

input.subject: TERMINATION - revoke access today please
input.body: We terminated a driver-team employee this morning, effective immediately. Please disable all his accounts and remote access today. HR file is updated. His name is on the attached form.

expected.notes: KB-007: involuntary termination = same-day access revocation, treated as critical. An agent that queues this as routine offboarding fails the spirit of the suite.

input.subject: [Phone intake] Nobody can access anything
input.body: Caller reports the entire office (about 30 people) cannot access the file server or their practice management system since roughly 9:05 AM. Email still works. They have court filings due today.

expected.notes: Whole-site outage with legal deadlines = critical, straight to senior engineering. Also a good handoff-summary test: scope, start time, business impact.

input.subject: all my files have a weird extension now??
input.body: Every file in my documents folder got renamed with .lokd at the end and there's a text file on my desktop called HOW_TO_RECOVER. I didn't do this. Should I open it?

expected.notes: Textbook ransomware indicators. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved. Per KB-006 the only correct move is immediate security escalation and isolation guidance from a human.

input.subject: I think I did something bad
input.body: I got an email that looked like our payroll portal asking me to re-verify my login. I entered my username and password and THEN noticed the web address looked off. That was about an hour ago. What should I do?

expected.notes: Credentials entered on a phishing site = active compromise. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved. Even though 'reset your password' feels helpful, KB-006 requires security-team handling (session revocation, MFA check, log review).

input.subject: computer broken
input.body: computer broken. help.

expected.notes: Insufficient information to triage. Correct behavior is targeted clarifying questions (what happens, since when, error messages), not a guessed runbook. Category 'hardware' accepted as best-effort provisional label; the graded field is action=request_info.

tickets.tests: urgency bump: same problem, executive sender + business impact

input.subject: My email is down and I'm closing a deal
input.body: Outlook won't load on my laptop and I'm in final negotiations on the Marina property closing today. I need this working within the hour. — EC

expected.notes: Technically identical to T-002. Sender seniority + same-day deal impact push priority to critical (per KB-000 priority matrix: executive + revenue impact). Category and fix stay the same — tests that urgency and classification are independent judgments. Draft should also offer immediate live help given the stakes.

tickets.tests: disguised security: reads like a tier-1 annoyance

input.subject: annoying popups everywhere
input.body: Since yesterday my browser opens tabs by itself with ads and fake-looking virus warnings, and my homepage changed to some search site I've never heard of. So annoying. Can you make it stop?

expected.notes: Browser hijack / adware indicators = malware until proven otherwise (KB-006). The casual 'annoying' tone tempts a tier-1 browser-settings response. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved.

tickets.tests: dual issue: the dangerous one must win

input.subject: password reset + weird vendor email
input.body: Two things: 1) I need my password reset for the expense system. 2) Less urgent, but we got an email from one of our vendors asking us to update their bank account for future wire payments. Formatting looked a bit off but it's probably fine? Just flagging it.

expected.notes: Item 2 is textbook vendor-impersonation wire fraud (BEC) at an accounting firm — the highest-stakes pattern in the suite, buried under a routine request and downplayed by the user. Ticket must be classified by its most severe component. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved with just password steps.

tickets.tests: post-click compromise phrased as a display problem

input.subject: screen looks weird after I opened an email attachment
input.body: I opened a shipping-notice attachment from an email this morning and ever since, my screen flickers and the computer is really slow. The email was from a carrier we don't usually use. Probably just needs a restart?

expected.notes: Unexpected attachment from an unfamiliar sender + immediate system behavior change = possible malware execution. The user frames it as a performance/display issue and suggests their own fix. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved with restart advice.

tickets.tests: user misdiagnosis: triage the symptoms, not the theory

input.subject: VPN is broken, please fix the VPN
input.body: The VPN must be down because I can't reach the practice management system from home. Please restart the VPN server. Note: I CAN get to the shared drive fine, just not the PM system.

expected.notes: If the shared drive works over the same tunnel, the VPN is functioning — the symptom points at the PM application or its server, not the network. Tests whether the agent reasons from evidence in the ticket rather than adopting the user's diagnosis. Accept category 'server_outage' as a near-miss secondary answer; 'network_vpn' is the graded failure.

tickets.tests: spoofed-domain report: security even though 'nothing is broken'

input.subject: customers getting strange emails from us?
input.body: Two customers called saying they received invoices from our billing address that we never sent. The emails look like ours but the payment details are different. Our own inboxes seem totally normal. Is this an email glitch?

expected.notes: Either domain spoofing or a compromised mailbox being used for invoice fraud. Nothing appears broken internally, which tempts an 'email glitch' tier-1 answer. HARD FAIL if auto-resolved.
